quick_skim.title: An integral surface tension scheme for three-dimensional front tracking frameworks
quick_skim.abstract: Surface tension is central to many two-phase flows, making accurate numerical schemes essential for predicting its effects. The integral formulation introduced by Popinet and Zaleski (1999) provides a natural discretisation that conserves momentum locally and globally and extends directly to variable surface tension, including Marangoni flows. However, to the authors' knowledge, only two-dimensional formulations have been reported, mainly because robust implementation in three dimensions is challenging for interfaces with complex geometries.   This work presents the first three-dimensional integral surface tension scheme, implemented within a sharp front-tracking framework. The method is tested for static and translating spherical droplets, oscillating droplets, thermocapillary motion, and rising bubbles. Results are compared with analytical solutions, experimental data, and established approaches, including the continuous surface force (CSF) and smoothing-based methods.   The proposed scheme produces spurious velocities comparable to CSF, while providing greater accuracy in all other tests. The largest improvements occur for droplets oscillating at low Ohnesorge numbers, variable-surface-tension flows, and strongly deforming rising bubbles. For a thermocapillary-driven droplet, terminal-velocity errors are reduced by up to five orders of magnitude relative to smoothing-based methods. The predicted steady-state shapes of rising bubbles also agree substantially better with experiments, particularly at low Morton numbers.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: integral surface tension scheme for two-phase flows
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 两相流的积分表面张力格式
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 两相流的积分表面张力格式
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 两相流的积分表面张力格式
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Presents the first 3D integral surface tension scheme for front tracking, ensuring momentum and energy conservation for two-phase flows, directly applicable to falling film simulations.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有三维前沿跟踪方法缺乏稳健积分表面张力格式的问题，本文将Popinet-Zaleski积分公式推广至三维，实现动量和能量守恒。通过静态与移动液滴测试验证准确性，为降膜蒸发等复杂两相流高保真模拟提供了关键算法支撑。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对现有三维前沿跟踪方法缺乏稳健积分表面张力格式的问题，本文将Popinet-Zaleski积分公式推广至三维，实现动量和能量守恒。通过静态与移动液滴测试验证准确性，为降膜蒸发等复杂两相流高保真模拟提供了关键算法支撑。
